description_long: This is a compact folding karambit knife with a pronounced curved blade, typically used for utility or self-defense. The blade is single-edged, featuring a dark metallic finish with a distinct color contrast near the tip. The handle is ergonomically shaped with multiple circular cutouts, likely for weight reduction and grip enhancement, and includes a prominent finger ring at the end for secure handling. The handle appears to be constructed from metal with visible screws and a textured grip section, possibly rubberized or ribbed for better control. The knife has a visible pivot point for folding, and a pocket clip is attached to one side for convenient carrying. The overall design is tactical and modern, with functional and aesthetic elements suited for urban environments.
description: A compact, curved-blade folding karambit knife with a finger ring, ergonomic handle, and pocket clip. The design is tactical, featuring a dark blade and metallic handle with grip-enhancing cutouts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved blade, finger ring, and handle cutouts.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thin profile and pocket clip attachment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade curve, handle structure, and finger ring.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the blade side, showing the blade's thickness and handle alignment.
attribute_handle_design: ergonomic, metallic with circular cutouts, finger ring, and pocket clip
